title: Social media is abuzz with weight-loss drug retatrutide and longevity goals. What is the drug all about?
summary: A new weight-loss drug, retatrutide, is generating excitement. This investigational medicine targets three hormone receptors, offering a potential breakthrough for diabetes and obesity. Experts believe it could also contribute to longevity goals. Clinical...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
Social media has been abuzz about retatrutide, a weight-loss drug that is in late clinical trials but is already being seen by medical experts as the next big leap in the treatment of diabetes and obesity.

Novo Nordisk’s Ozempic (semaglutide) lost its patent protection last month, prompting a raft of Indian companies to launch at least 20 generic brands for a share of the rapidly unfolding market which has already reached Rs 1,600 crore. US-based Eli Lilly’s Mounjaro (tirzepatide) is leading the charts, clocking the highest sales among all weight-loss drugs sold in India so far.

Also Read | Obesity lab: India becomes ground zero for weight-loss drug race

But there are more advanced drugs in the research pipeline which are set to alter the medical care paradigm for metabolic conditions such as diabetes and heart disease. Experts say that the next generation of drugs may not just benefit patients in controlling a range of conditions but their larger impact can also help achieve longevity or anti-ageing goals for millions around the world. The most advanced drug in trial stages at present is retatrutide, a once-weekly injection, from Eli Lilly, the maker of blockbuster medicines Mounjaro and Zepbound (tirzepatide).

What makes retatrutide unique? Here is an explainer on why doctors are calling it a potential game changer even before it has been approved by the regulatory agencies like the US Food and Drug Administration (US FDA).

What is retatrutide?

Retatrutide is an investigational once-weekly triple hormone receptor agonist. Simply put, it is a single drug that activates the body’s receptors for glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP), glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1), and glucagon. The drug is therefore referred to as Triple-G by doctors and scientists.

These three receptor hormones are involved in regulating appetite, energy balance and metabolism.

Eli Lilly is currently studying retatrutide in several phase-3 clinical trials for obesity, type 2 diabetes, knee osteoarthritis pain, moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea, chronic low back pain, cardiovascular and renal outcomes, and metabolic dysfunction-associated steatosis liver disease.

Why do biohackers associate retatrutide with longevity and wellness?

According to Dr. Rajiv Kovil, a medical expert in diabetes and weight-loss drugs, the use of peptides and tools like genomics as new interventions is being closely watched. Retatrutide could be the “biggest molecule”, according to him, because of its action on the three receptors. In retatrutide, the markers related to longevity or ageing can be seen as a therapy. It can convert a weight-loss drug from a caloric restriction model to a metabolic engineering model. Besides, experts say there could be a large cohort of patients who have not benefited from semaglutide or tirzepatide and may benefit from retatrutide.

How is retatrutide different from other incretins or weight-loss drugs in the market?

Currently available incretin (metabolic hormones) therapies activate either the GLP-1 receptor or both GIP and GLP-1 receptors, but glucagon is the additional edge in retatrutide. There have been no head-to-head studies to-date comparing retatrutide with other incretins, and experts caution that owing to differences in clinical study designs and patient populations it is not appropriate to draw conclusions from cross-trial comparisons.

How does adding glucagon to GLP-1 and GIP help?

Glucagon is demonstrated to increase energy expenditure. Further, glucagon reduces lipid synthesis and fat accumulation in the liver, providing a unique combination of benefits to control liver fat and serum lipid levels.

How exactly do the three metabolic receptors work?

These drugs are approved for use as an adjunct with diet and exercise.

GLP-1 drugs act on satiety control and work on the brain regions to control appetite and slow gastric emptying, thereby helping reduce glucose in the blood.

GIP drugs, like Mounjaro, enhance insulin secretion and improve fat tissue metabolism. They have been seen to have some effect on pleasure-derived eating, or hedonic hyperphagia, thereby helping lower the cravings to eat.

Glucagon, which is added in retatrutide, is the next-generation drug. It increases energy expenditure and reduces liver fat. Dr. Kovil says in a phase-2 study it showed an almost 90-95% reduction in liver fat. So retatrutide can reduce food intake while also increasing energy burn. That has made it so popular among longevity influencers and advocates.Also Read: Easy access to weight-loss drugs rings alarm bells among doctors, authorities; regulators step in

What do the latest clinical trials reveal about the weight-loss properties of retatrutide?

In March, Eli Lilly announced results of TRASCEND T2D-1 – its phase-3 study on retatrutide. For a key secondary endpoint, participants taking the drug lost up to an average of 16.6 kg (16.8%) at 40 weeks. The loss of weight continued through the end of the treatment period.

In December 2025, Eli Lilly announced topline results from the phase-3 TRIUMPH-4 clinical trial evaluating the safety and efficacy of retatrutide in adults with obesity or overweight and knee osteoarthritis, and without diabetes, as an adjunct to healthy diet and physical activity. Retatrutide lowered weight by up to an average of 28.7% (32.2 kgs) in 68 weeks – the highest among all weight-loss drugs - and reduced knee osteoarthritis pain by an average of 4.5 points (75.8%) using the validated pain score tool WOMAC (Western Ontario and McMaster Universities Osteoarthritis Index).

But Dr. Kovil highlights another path-breaking finding from that trial. He says while a plateauing of weight-loss is seen in Mounjaro (tirzepatide) at around 10-12 weeks and semaglutide plateaued at around 45-50 weeks, retatrutide showed weight-reduction benefits even after 68 weeks, the end time for the clinical trial. That means the drug may have weight-loss benefits beyond its expected duration.

Can retatrutide be seen as an alternative to bariatric surgery?

Dr. Kovil emphasises that it can be even better than bariatric surgery. By preventing plateauing, retatrutide can be a powerful intervention against fat oxidation while getting better energy burn via glucagon. Besides, he says there are several disease modifying effects that can make retatrutide a big drug.

Does retatrutide lead to lean muscle loss?

Retatrutide leads to significant weight loss with some reduction in lean mass, consistent with physiological weight-loss patterns. Current evidence suggests fat loss predominates but Dr. Kovil adds that, importantly, muscle preservation depends on adequate protein intake and resistance training. The drug doesn't block muscle production. Experts say they have not observed any concerning signs of muscle weakness in clinical trials to date. Drug makers are currently working on targets such as amylin, which preserves lean muscle while aiding fat loss.

How does retatrutide help in knee osteoarthritis?

People with obesity and knee osteoarthritis often have pain and restricted mobility. In TRIUMPH-4, participants taking retatrutide achieved substantial weight loss and reported improvements in pain and physical function compared to placebo. These findings are encouraging, and with seven additional phase-3 readouts expected in 2026, Eli Lilly told ET that the company believes retatrutide could become an important option for patients with significant weight-loss needs and certain complications, including knee osteoarthritis.

When is retatrutide expected to be submitted for the US FDA approval and its launch?

Eli Lilly plans to submit retatrutide application by the end of 2026 based on the TRIUMPH trials in obesity, knee osteoarthritis pain and moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea. Submissions for type 2 diabetes will follow in 2027. Additional results from the clinical trial programme are expected over the next year.

What does the Eli Lilly research pipeline for weight-loss drugs look like?

Eli Lilly has 11 medicines in clinical trials that could potentially treat a wide variety of cardiometabolic diseases. Apart from retatrutide, the late-stage incretin portfolio includes other mode of action approaches such as the oral GLP-1, orforglipron, a weekly basal insulin efsitora and muvalaplin.

Eli Lilly says the next phase of clinical development focuses on several comorbidity diseases associated with obesity. In the past few decades, the company pursued the goal of using genetic medicine to address targets that were previously “undruggable” and to “fix” genetic anomalies that underlie disease. These therapies have the potential to transform treatment paradigms for patients.
